generatedBy: LLM-assisted (see prompts.md for exact prompts used)

testCases.title: Successful login with correct username and password
preconditions: A registered, active user account exists
User is on the Login page
testCases.expectedResult: User is authenticated and redirected to the Dashboard; a valid session token/cookie is issued.

testCases.title: Successful login with 'Remember Me' checked
preconditions: A registered, active user account exists
testCases.expectedResult: User remains logged in on browser reopen; a persistent session/token is stored with an extended expiry.

testCases.title: Login is case-insensitive for username/email field
preconditions: A registered account exists with email 'User@Example.com'
steps: Enter the email in a different case than registered
testCases.expectedResult: Login succeeds; email matching is case-insensitive (per application's defined behavior).

testCases.title: Login fails with incorrect password
preconditions: A registered account exists
testCases.expectedResult: Login is rejected; a generic error message is shown (e.g., 'Invalid username or password'); no session is created.
testCases.notes: Error message must not reveal whether the username or password was the incorrect field (security best practice).

testCases.title: Login fails with non-existent username
preconditions: Username is not registered in the system
testCases.expectedResult: Login is rejected with the same generic error message as an incorrect password (no user enumeration).

testCases.title: Login fails with empty username and/or password fields
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: Leave username and password fields empty
testCases.expectedResult: Client-side and/or server-side validation blocks submission; required-field error messages are displayed.

testCases.title: Login rejects SQL injection / script injection attempts in credential fields
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: Enter SQL/script injection payloads into username and password
testCases.expectedResult: Input is safely sanitized/escaped; login fails normally; no script executes and no database error is exposed.

testCases.title: User can request a password reset with a registered email
preconditions: A registered account exists
testCases.expectedResult: A password reset email is sent to the address containing a valid, time-limited reset link/token; confirmation message is shown to the user.

testCases.title: Forgot password request with unregistered email shows neutral confirmation
preconditions: Email is not registered
steps: Navigate to the Forgot Password page
Enter an email not associated with any account
testCases.expectedResult: System shows the same generic confirmation message as a valid request (e.g., 'If an account exists, a reset link has been sent') without revealing whether the account exists.

testCases.title: Password reset link expires after configured time window
preconditions: A valid reset link was generated more than the expiry window ago (e.g., >30 min)
steps: Request a password reset and obtain the reset link
Wait until the token expiry window has passed
Attempt to set a new password
testCases.expectedResult: System rejects the expired token, displays an appropriate error, and prompts the user to request a new reset link.

testCases.title: Reset link/token can only be used once
preconditions: A valid, unused reset link exists
steps: Use the reset link to successfully change the password
Attempt to reuse the same reset link again
testCases.expectedResult: Second attempt is rejected with an 'invalid or expired token' error; token is invalidated after first use.

testCases.title: New password must meet complexity requirements during reset
preconditions: User has clicked a valid reset link
steps: Open a valid password reset link
Enter a weak password (e.g., too short, no special character)
testCases.expectedResult: System rejects the weak password and displays password policy requirements; password is not updated.

testCases.title: User is logged out automatically after session timeout due to inactivity
preconditions: User is logged in
Session timeout is configured (e.g., 15 minutes of inactivity)
steps: Remain inactive (no clicks, requests) for longer than the session timeout period
Attempt to perform an action or refresh the page
testCases.expectedResult: User is redirected to the login page with a 'Session expired, please log in again' message; the expired session token is invalidated server-side.

testCases.title: Expired session token is rejected on API calls
preconditions: User has an expired session/JWT token
steps: Send an API request to a protected endpoint using the expired token
testCases.expectedResult: API responds with 401 Unauthorized; response body indicates token expiry; no protected data is returned.

testCases.title: Session remains active during continuous user activity
preconditions: User is logged in
Session timeout is configured
steps: Perform an action (click/navigate) at intervals shorter than the timeout window, repeated across multiple cycles
testCases.expectedResult: Session stays active and is not terminated as long as activity continues before each timeout window elapses.

testCases.title: Manually logging out invalidates the session immediately
preconditions: User is logged in
steps: Attempt to navigate back using the browser back button or reuse the old session token
testCases.expectedResult: User is redirected to the login page; the old session token is invalidated and cannot be reused to access protected pages/APIs.

testCases.title: Account is locked after N consecutive failed login attempts
preconditions: A registered account exists
Lockout threshold is configured (e.g., 5 failed attempts)
steps: Enter the correct username with an incorrect password
Repeat the failed login attempt until the configured threshold is reached (e.g., 5 times)
testCases.expectedResult: After the threshold is reached, the account is locked; further login attempts (even with correct credentials) are rejected with a lockout message.

testCases.title: Locked account cannot log in even with correct credentials
preconditions: Account has already been locked due to prior failed attempts
steps: Ensure the target account is in a locked state
Attempt to log in using the correct, valid credentials
testCases.expectedResult: Login is rejected with a message indicating the account is temporarily locked; correct credentials do not bypass the lockout.

testCases.title: Account automatically unlocks after the configured lockout duration
preconditions: Account is locked
Lockout duration is configured (e.g., 30 minutes)
steps: Trigger account lockout via repeated failed attempts
Wait until the lockout duration has elapsed
Attempt to log in with correct credentials
testCases.expectedResult: Login succeeds after the lockout window expires, confirming automatic unlock.

testCases.title: Failed attempt counter resets after a successful login
preconditions: Account has some failed attempts below the lockout threshold
steps: Perform fewer failed login attempts than the lockout threshold (e.g., 3 out of 5)
Log in successfully with correct credentials
Perform failed login attempts again and verify the counter started fresh from zero
testCases.expectedResult: Successful login resets the failed attempt counter; account is not locked prematurely on the next round of failures.

testCases.title: Rate limiting / CAPTCHA is triggered after repeated failed attempts from the same IP
preconditions: Multiple failed login attempts originate from the same IP/client across one or more accounts
steps: Send repeated failed login requests rapidly from the same IP address, potentially across different usernames
Observe system response after the configured threshold
testCases.expectedResult: System applies rate limiting, throttling, or CAPTCHA challenge to the offending IP/client to mitigate brute-force/credential-stuffing attacks.
